bullets.text: Hamas has rejected a Gaza disarmament plan, with a Palestinian official stating that the group will not proceed with talks until Israel fully complies with its commitments.

The UK is set to advocate for an end to the Sudan bloodshed at Berlin talks, coinciding with the third anniversary of the war, while expressing concerns about the worsening humanitarian crisis and insufficient global efforts.

The Swiss National Bank has cut interest rates by a half point to 0.5%, while Federal Reserve Chair Powell indicated that a rate cut is 'on the table' as soon as September, noting that the labor market is not a significant source of inflation pressure.
US President Donald Trump has stated that peace talks with Iran could resume within the next two days, potentially in Islamabad under the Pakistani army chief.
King Charles is scheduled to have tea and a private meeting with US President Donald Trump during his upcoming state visit to the United States.
Latitude Financial has been fined millions of dollars for repeatedly breaching spam laws, including promoting credit cards without providing an opt-out option to recipients.

Tankers have stopped or turned around in the Strait of Hormuz amid a US blockade, with the US military reporting no ships passing through its operation to seal off Iranian ports in the first 24 hours.
Gold prices have held gains on optimism that the US and Iran are seeking a negotiated settlement to the war, easing inflation concerns arising from an energy-supply shock.
The third Gulf war is expected to scar energy markets for a long time, with residual risks and ruined infrastructure anticipated to keep prices high.
